cs-1.title: Case Study — Infrastructure & Aerospace Portfolio
paragraphs: The organization is a global engineering and manufacturing company that delivers both public infrastructure and aerospace products. You are the project manager for a portfolio that includes a major highway corridor expansion and a new aircraft development program.

The highway expansion is in the construction phase under a predictive life cycle. A detailed project management plan was approved by the PMO and is being executed with third-party contractors on site. During construction, the highway laborers' union raised safety concerns about working conditions and protective measures at several active work zones. Union representatives asked for immediate attention before work continued in the affected sections.

In parallel, the aircraft program is completing final certification activities. During the final inspection, the project manager was notified that the standard airworthiness certificate might be delayed because of outstanding documentation from a supplier subsystem. The certification authority controls the schedule; the project team cannot fast-track regulatory approval.

Stakeholders are concerned that expenses are already higher than planned. They expect project processes to align with company standards, regulations, and policies, and they want deliverables and quality activities to reflect the approved project vision. The PMO reviews project management plan components before execution proceeds.

cs-2.title: Case Study — Post-Merger Software Integration
paragraphs: Two software companies have recently merged. You are the project manager leading integration of their engineering teams on a shared product platform. The combined team includes software engineers from both legacy organizations, each with different tools, practices, and specialized technical knowledge.

The project uses an adaptive approach with a working prototype demonstrated to the project sponsor. The sponsor is dissatisfied with the current outcome and wants clearer visibility into priorities and progress. Team members have reported fatigue from long hours and recurring process issues that are slowing delivery.

Knowledge is siloed between the former companies. Critical expertise is not flowing across teams, and informal communication has not been enough to transfer specialized skills. The project manager must improve collaboration, establish feedback loops during execution, and manage sponsor expectations while the team continues to refine the prototype.

cs-3.title: Case Study — Manufacturing Program Under Budget Pressure
paragraphs: You are managing a manufacturing program that has experienced cost growth beyond the original forecast. Senior stakeholders are scrutinizing spending and asking how the budget will be managed going forward.

The organization has established governance policies that incorporate regulatory and ethical requirements into project execution. The project must comply with those policies while preparing for uncertainties that could affect cost and schedule.
Financial forecasts need to stay current as work progresses so the team can take corrective actions when variances emerge.

cs-4.title: Case Study — Manufacturing Workflow Improvement
paragraphs: A manufacturing project team recently adopted a new digital tool that reduced time spent on administrative tasks. Lessons learned from the rollout showed measurable efficiency gains in routine reporting and coordination work.
The project manager is reviewing how those improvements were achieved so similar methods can be applied to other workflows across the program.

cs-5.title: Case Study — IT Platform With Security Incident
paragraphs: You are leading an IT platform project with aggressive timeline and budget targets. Stakeholders have raised concerns after unexpected changes and external disruptions affected progress.

During execution, the IT department reported a security concern related to a potential safety breach in the project environment. The issue may affect priorities, resources, and stakeholder confidence. The project manager must assess impact, reprioritize work, and communicate options while maintaining schedule where possible.

cs-6.title: Case Study — Corporate Merger Integration
paragraphs: Two large organizations have merged. You are the project manager for an integration program that must align teams, processes, and expected outcomes with the combined company's values and strategic direction.

Team members come from distinct organizational cultures with different communication norms and ways of working. Stakeholders are navigating significant change and need guidance through merger-related impacts on scope, priorities, and deliverables.

A foreign government has recently expressed interest in acquiring an ownership stake in the newly merged company. Leadership is evaluating how external investment could change strategic priorities, compliance requirements, and project scope assumptions.
